description: Cross-App Agentic Intelligence - Wire quant-tools orchestrator into QuantAI with cross-app action execution, tool execution cards, multi-step workflow support, and AI context awareness. This is the core differentiator: unified AI that executes actions across all apps.
steps: Step 1: Create packages/quant-tools/src/orchestrator/cross-app-orchestrator.ts - A CrossAppOrchestrator class that uses IntentRouter + MultiStepPlanner + ToolExecutor together. It takes a natural language command, routes to the right tools, creates a plan, executes steps, and returns structured results with progress callbacks. Should support streaming progress via an EventEmitter pattern.

Step 2: Create packages/quant-tools/src/orchestrator/context-manager.ts - An AI context manager that tracks which app the user is in, what content they're viewing, recent actions, and can inject this context into tool calls. Stores app context (currentApp, currentItem, recentItems) and resolves references like 'this email' or 'current document'.
Step 3: Create packages/quant-tools/src/orchestrator/index.ts barrel export and update packages/quant-tools/src/index.ts to re-export the orchestrator classes.

Step 4: Create apps/quantai/src/app/api/ask/route.ts - The 'Ask Quant' API endpoint that accepts natural language commands, uses CrossAppOrchestrator to plan and execute, streams progress via SSE (reusing the existing streaming pattern from apps/quantai/src/app/api/orchestrate/route.ts). Handles commands like 'Send email to X', 'Schedule meeting for tomorrow', 'Upload file to drive', 'Post update on QuantSync'.

Step 5: Create apps/quantai/src/components/AskQuantPanel.tsx - A React component that provides the 'Ask Quant' conversational interface. Shows the input, streaming AI response, tool execution cards (reusing ToolCallCard component pattern), and multi-step workflow progress. Uses framer-motion for card enter/exit animations. Dark mode via CSS vars.

Step 6: Create apps/quantai/src/components/WorkflowProgressCard.tsx - Shows multi-step workflow progress with step-by-step status (pending/running/completed/failed), elapsed time, and results preview for each step. Used when orchestrator executes multi-tool plans.

Step 7: Create packages/quant-tools/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts - Tests for CrossAppOrchestrator: plan creation from natural language, context-aware tool selection, multi-step execution with dependency resolution, error handling for failed steps, streaming progress events.
Step 8: Create apps/quantai/src/__tests__/ask-quant-panel.test.tsx - Component test for AskQuantPanel verifying render, input handling, streaming display, tool card rendering.
acceptance_criteria: CrossAppOrchestrator takes natural language input and produces a ToolPlan with correct tool selection
Context manager correctly tracks app state and resolves references
The /api/ask SSE endpoint streams tool execution progress events
AskQuantPanel renders with input, shows streaming response, displays tool execution cards
WorkflowProgressCard shows multi-step progress with correct status indicators
All new tests pass via vitest run
TypeScript strict mode compiles without errors
findings: Added @quant/quant-tools as workspace dependency to quantai app (package.json + tsconfig path mapping). Prettier ran via pre-commit hook and reformatted some files. All 73 quant-tools tests and 191 quantai tests pass. Build and typecheck successful.
